elements.sent-2411-001: Abstract:~ We study the max-type recursive model introduced by Hu and Shi (J.. Stat.
elements.sent-2411-002: Phys., 2018), which generalizes the model of Derrida and Retaux (J.. Stat.
elements.sent-2411-003: We show that the class of geometric-type distributions are preserved by the model with geometric offspring distribution.
elements.sent-2411-004: The key result is a characterization for the long-time asymptotic behavior of the marginal distributions.
elements.sent-2411-005: From this result, we derive the conditional limit law and the asymptotics of the sustainability probability and the first moment in the critical case.
elements.sent-2411-006: Keywords and phrases:~ Generalized Derrida-Retaux model, geometric offspring distribution, geometric-type distribution, sustainability probability, asymptotic behavior 2020 Mathematics Subject Classification:~ 60J05, 82B27.
elements.sent-2411-007: Let $X_0$ be a random variable taking values in $\mbb{Z _+:= \{0,1,2,\cdots\}$.}
elements.sent-2411-008: By a Derrida-Retaux process, or DR process for short, we mean a sequence of random variables $(X_n: n\ge 0)$ defined recursively in the distribution sense by \beqlb X_{n+1}\overset{\rm d = (X_n+\tilde{X_n -1)_+, \quad n\geq 0, \eeqlb where $(z)_+:= \max(0,z)$ and $\tilde{X _n$ is an independent copy of $X_n$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-009: By \eqref{DR0proc it is easy to see that \beqnn \mbf{E (X_{n+1 )\le 2\mbf{E (X_n).}}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: The DR process is referred to as pinned if $F_\infty> 0$, and as unpinned if $F_\infty= 0$.
elements.sent-2411-011: One main problem in this study is to determine for which distribution of $X_0$ the model is pinned or unpinned.
elements.sent-2411-012: Another basic question is the asymptotic behavior of the sustainability probability $\mbf{P (X_n\ge 1)$ as $n\to \infty$.}
elements.sent-2411-013: The model is said to be critical if $\mbf{E (2^{X_0 )= \mbf{E (X_02^{X_0 )< \infty$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: In this case, it is expected that \beqlb \mbf{P (X_n\ge 1)= \frac{4 {n^2 + o\Big(\frac{1 {n^2}\Big), \quad n\to \infty.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-015: A number of variations of the DR process have also been studied.
elements.sent-2411-016: Let $\eta$ be a random variable taking values in $\{1,2,\cdots\}$.
elements.sent-2411-017: Instead of \eqref{DR0proc , we can also define a discrete-time max-type recursive process $(Y_n: n\ge 0)$ by the formula \beqlb Y_{n+1}\overset{\rm d = (Y_{n,1 +Y_{n,2 +\cdots+Y_{n,\eta -1)_+, \eeqlb where $\{Y_{n,1 , Y_{n,2 , \ldots\}$ is a sequence of independent copies of $Y_n$ independent of $\eta$.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-018: The model was first studied by Hu and Shi \cite{HuS18 .}
elements.sent-2411-019: Following \cite{HuS18 , we call $(Y_n: n\ge 0)$ a generalized DR process.}
elements.sent-2411-020: A scaling limit theorem for the process was proved in \cite{LiZ24 , which leads to a generalization of the continuous-time Derrida--Retaux process introduced by Hu et al.\ \cite{HMP20 .}}

elements.sent-2411-133: Keywords:~ Generalized Derrida--Retaux model, geometric offspring distribution, geometric-type distribution, sustainability probability, first moment, generating function, asymptotic behavior Mathematics Subject Classification:~ 60J05, 82B27.
elements.sent-2411-134: By a Derrida--Retaux process, or DR process for short, we mean a discrete-time stochastic process $(X_n: n\ge 0)$ defined recursively in the distribution sense by \beqlb X_{n+1}\overset{\rm d = (X_n+\tilde{X _n-1)_+, \quad n\geq 0, \eeqlb where $(\,z\,)_+:= \max(0,z)$ and $\tilde{X _n$ is an independent copy of $X_n$.}}}
